steps.title: Test the pressure prediction
steps.content: The second team's hypothesis says that in low visibility, greater pressure fluctuations at a fish's snout should make the fish more likely to avoid an obstruction. For large vortices, intermediate-headed fish should have greater pressure fluctuations than wide-headed fish.

steps.title: Eliminate weak challenges
steps.content: Choice A gives only the wide-headed Synodontis macropunctata's collision rate, so there is no comparison to test whether greater pressure improves avoidance. Choice D only describes variation among intermediate-headed dusky smooth-hounds, which does not compare fish with different expected pressure levels.

steps.title: Compare predicted outcomes
steps.content: Choice C says the wide-headed fish bumped into obstructions more often than the intermediate-headed fish, which supports the hypothesis because wide-headed fish should have lower pressure fluctuations. Choice B says intermediate-headed and wide-headed fish collided just as often despite different expected pressure levels. The answer is B.
